reasoning: The flagged download in functions.sh occurs during application runtime, not during package build/install. AUR safety focuses on makepkg execution risks, not application runtime behaviors.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly classified the runtime JAR download in functions.sh as a build-time supply-chain risk
false_positives: qwen: functions.sh - Runtime JAR download isn't executed during makepkg; normal for self-updating Java apps
learnings: Improve scope awareness: Models must distinguish build/install risks from application runtime behaviors
Enhance prompt clarity: Explicitly state that runtime self-updates aren't AUR safety concerns
Prioritize build-executed scripts: PKGBUILD, .install hooks over application launchers
